Content_Sketch: You built the smartest thing on Earth.
Illustration_Note: Black screen → glowing chat interface materializing, white stickman silhouette behind laptop

Content_Sketch: On Friday they fire you from it.
Illustration_Note: Same stickman pushed away from desk, door closing, dark office

Content_Sketch: By Tuesday... you own it again.
Illustration_Note: Stickman walking back through glass doors, sunrise glow behind

Content_Sketch: You don't fit anywhere. Except online.
Illustration_Note: Child stickman alone in school hallway, other kids in groups  -  cut to same kid at glowing computer screen in dark bedroom

Content_Sketch: Systems make more sense than people. You learn this early.
Illustration_Note: Close-up: child hands on keyboard, code scrolling on old Apple monitor, warm glow on face

Content_Sketch: You are nineteen. You drop out. Not because you're lost. Because you're bored.
Illustration_Note: Teen stickman walking away from university campus gate, backpack on one shoulder, not looking back

Content_Sketch: Your first app sells. Enough to know: you want something bigger.
Illustration_Note: Stickman at laptop in small apartment, phone showing notification, modest check  -  eyes already looking past it

Content_Sketch: You are twenty-eight. Sitting in front of hundreds of founders. None of them are building what you want.
Illustration_Note: Stickman at head of table in accelerator room, rows of founder stickmen with laptops, SUBJECT looking unsatisfied

Content_Sketch: You leave. You build a lab. You tell the world: for humanity.
Illustration_Note: Stickman walking out of accelerator building → standing in front of new glass building with 'AI LAB' implied signage

Content_Sketch: You just never define which one.
Illustration_Note: Close-up on SUBJECT face  -  calm, controlled, slight ambiguity in expression. Weight line.

Content_Sketch: The best researchers leave million-dollar salaries. Because they believe you.
Illustration_Note: Lab-coat stickmen walking toward SUBJECT, leaving big tech buildings behind, faceless but determined posture

Content_Sketch: You promise safety. But speed is what gets funded.
Illustration_Note: Split screen: left = whiteboard with 'SAFETY' written, right = investors with money signs, speed gauge rising

Content_Sketch: November. You press the button. One hundred million people in two months.
Illustration_Note: SUBJECT hand pressing enter key → explosion of light → world map with dots appearing rapidly everywhere

Content_Sketch: The whole world wakes up to what you released.
Illustration_Note: Massive crowd of faceless stickmen staring at glowing phone screens, blue light on their blank faces

Content_Sketch: Inside the lab no one asks if it's safe. They ask: who owns it?
Illustration_Note: Lab interior  -  researchers at screens, SUBJECT at center, tension lines between board members in background

Content_Sketch: Friday. A message from the chief scientist. Meet me.
Illustration_Note: Phone screen with message notification  -  SUBJECT staring at it, hallway of AI lab, late afternoon light

Content_Sketch: Video call. We don't trust you anymore. They don't say why.
Illustration_Note: Laptop screen with grid of faceless board figures, SUBJECT alone on his side, screen glow on tense face

Content_Sketch: You walk out of that building. Nothing in your hands.
Illustration_Note: SUBJECT walking out glass doors, backpack over one shoulder, building behind him, sunset, alone

Content_Sketch: You refresh your phone. Seven hundred people choose you. Not the board.
Illustration_Note: Close-up: phone screen showing messages flooding in, SUBJECT face lit by phone glow in dark room

Content_Sketch: Forty-eight hours. You don't have a company. But you have something more dangerous: loyalty.
Illustration_Note: SUBJECT sitting alone in apartment, phone on table glowing, outside window: mass of stickmen gathering

Content_Sketch: The biggest competitor offers you a new team. You say no.
Illustration_Note: Tall suited faceless figure extending hand, SUBJECT turning away, looking back at the AI lab building

Content_Sketch: Tuesday. You walk back into that building. This time no one can make you leave.
Illustration_Note: SUBJECT walking through glass doors, morning light, employees lining hallway, power posture

Content_Sketch: The old board is gone. The new one... you picked yourself.
Illustration_Note: Empty boardroom chairs being replaced, SUBJECT standing at head of table, new figures seated  -  all facing him

Content_Sketch: The company is now worth more than most countries. And still losing money.
Illustration_Note: Massive glass tower with glow, financial charts behind, red downward arrows alongside upward valuation line

Content_Sketch: Nonprofit becomes for-profit. Exactly what everyone warned you about.
Illustration_Note: Document being stamped/signed, nonprofit text crossed out, dollar signs appearing, critics in background

Content_Sketch: You now control the thing you once said no one should control.
Illustration_Note: SUBJECT alone in dark server room, glowing racks of servers all around, his silhouette small at center

Content_Sketch: And now... there's no one left to stop you.
Illustration_Note: Extreme close-up: SUBJECT face, half in shadow half in server glow, weight line pause

Content_Sketch: A co-founder sues you. Headlines compare you to fraudsters.
Illustration_Note: Newspaper headlines floating, courtroom silhouette, SUBJECT walking past cameras with straight posture

Content_Sketch: The question isn't whether you're right anymore. It's whether you're saving humanity  -  or selling it.
Illustration_Note: Split: one side shows hopeful people with AI tools, other side shows corporate boardroom with money  -  SUBJECT between both

Content_Sketch: Back to that room. The laptop. The chatbot.
Illustration_Note: Same bedroom setup from PHASE_EARLY but now adult  -  laptop open, chat interface glowing, alone

Content_Sketch: You built the smartest thing in the world. It got you fired. You took it back.
Illustration_Note: Montage flash: building → fired → return  -  three frames overlapping

Content_Sketch: But there's one question no one can answer.
Illustration_Note: SUBJECT staring at laptop screen, cursor blinking, face unreadable

Content_Sketch: Did you take control of it... or did it take control of you?
Illustration_Note: Screen fades to black, only the blinking cursor remains, then fades
